Member-only story

In a distributed team, spec as single source of truth replaces the meeting. The mechanism, async spec review, five principles, and metrics for remote leads.

12 min read
1 hour ago

--

Everyone believes alignment in a distributed team is maintained through meetings — but across many time zones, the meeting where everyone agreed is the most expensive illusion you can buy. This applies to anyone running remote: decisions made on a video call degrade the moment the team disperses to their tasks, and distributed tasks take on average two and a half times longer than the same work done in one place. Over the next ten minutes, you’ll see why a specification as a single source of truth is structurally suited to distributed work, what a good async spec review looks like — and five principles plus the metrics to verify that everyone’s building the same thing.
“The greatest enemy of communication is the illusion of it.” — William H. Whyte, writer and Fortune editor, September 1950
That sentence from seventy-five years ago describes exactly the pathology of a distributed team. The meeting happens, heads nod, cameras go off — and that’s precisely when the drift begins, invisible to everyone…
